properties.remark: AT 1106 EST...MONROE COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTED A TELEVISION CABLE LINE DOWN AND BLOCKING THE ROAD AT 112TH STREET OCEAN IN MARATHON.
ELECTRICAL LINES WERE REPORTED DOWN BY A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SIMONTON AND FLEMING ST IN THE CITY OF KEY WEST.
ELECTRICAL LINES CONFIRMED DOWN AT THE 1600 BLOCK OF SEMINARY STREET IN THE CITY OF KEY WEST.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE NORTHWEST WAS OBSERVED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT CMAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING SQUALL LINE.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE NORTHWEST WAS REPORTED AT THE MOLASSES REEF LIGHT CMAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING SQUALL LINE.
A WIND GUST OF 44 KNOTS...OR 51 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT LONG KEY LIGHT. THIS WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.

A NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT LOCATED ABOUT 5 TO 7 MILES EAST SOUTHEAST OF TAVERNIER CREEK. THE WATERSPOUT FORMED WITHIN A CUMULUS CLOUD-LINE WITH THE VISIBILE FUNNEL EXTENDING DOWN TO AN OBSTRUCED VIEW BEHIND A TREE LINE ALONG THE HORIZON. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS...OR 48 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 48 KTS...OR 55 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT AT 1238 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING SQUALL LINE.
A WIND GUST OF 38 KTS...OR 44 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON LONG KEY LIGHT AT 1247 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING SQUALL LINE.
A NORTH-NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 37 KTS...OR 43 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT...AT 100 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING SQUALL LINE.
A WINDGUST OF 37 KNOTS...OR 43 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A GUST FRONT AND THUNDERSTORMS MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS...OR 48 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT LONG KEY LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A GUST FRONT AND THUNDERSTORMS MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 48 KNOTS...OR 55 MPH WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A GUST FRONT AND THUNDERSTORMS MOVING THROUG THE AREA.
A NORTH-NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 41 KTS...OR 47 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON MOLASSES REEF LIGHT AT 108 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING SQUALL LINE.
A NORTH-NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 46 KTS...OR 53 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT AT 110 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING SQUALL LINE.
A NORTH-NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 37 KTS...OR 43 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON LONG KEY LIGHT...AT 126 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING SQUALL LINE.
A NORTH-NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 35 KTS...OR 40 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT AT 200 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING SQUALL LINE.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE CITIZENS WEATHER OBSERVER PROGRAM MESONET SENSOR AT THE FLORIDA KEYS AQUEDUCT AUTHORITY BUILDING ON LONG KEY.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE CITIZENS WEATHER OBSERVER PROGRAM MESONET SENSOR ON CUDJOE KEY.
A NORTHEAST WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...OR 43 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT ASSOCIATED WITH A GUST FRONT NEAR A THUNDERSTORM.
A NORTH WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS...OR 45 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE CITIZENS WEATHER OBSERVER PROGRAM MESONET SENSOR AT THE FLORIDA KEYS AQUEDUCT AUTHORITY BUILDING ON LONG KEY.
A NORTH-NORTHEAST WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT MOLASSES REEF LIGHT.
A NORTHEAST WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS...OR 44 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SAND KEY LIGHT.
A SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 54 KNOTS...OR 62 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT MOLASSES REEF LIGHT.
A SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT LONG KEY LIGHT.

A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED TWO WATERSPOUTS LOCATED ABOUT 5 MILES OFFSHORE OF MARATHON ON THE GULF SIDE. ONE WATERSPOUT LOCATED DUE NORTH OF 33RD STREET IN MARATHON EXTENDED TO THE SURFACE AND HAD A LARGE VISIBLE SPRAY RING. THE SECOND WATERSPOUT WAS LOCATED ABOUT HALF A MILE TO THE EAST AND LASTED ONLY A FEW MINUTES WITH A VISIBLE FUNNEL EXTENDING HALF WAY TO THE WATER SURFACE. DURATION OF THE WATERSPOUT NORTH OF 33RD STREET WAS 11 MINUTES.

LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED 2 WATERSPOUTS EAST NORTHEAST OF SNAKE CREEK NEAR MILE MARKER 86 OVER THE ATLANTIC WATERS 1 TO 2 MILES OFFSHORE. ONE WATERSPOUT QUICKLY DISSIPATED AND THE OTHER LARGER WATERSPOUT LASTED ABOUT 28 MINUTES.

A WATERSPOUT WAS SITED SOUTH OF COW KEY CHANNEL BY A NWS OBSERVER. DURATION OF SPOUT WAS APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES. SEVERAL FUNNEL CLOUDS WERE ALSO SEEN PROTRUDING FROM THE BASE OF THE CUMULUS LINE THAT WAS DEVELOPING ALONG SOUTHERN SIDE OF THE LOWER KEYS ISLAND CHAIN. CUMULUS LINE TRANSLATED SLOWLY TO SW.
TRAINED SPOTTER BOB WIESE...SPOTTER 91...REPORTED 2 WATERSPOUTS ABOUT 2 MILES SOUTH OF KEY WEST...ONE WHICH WAS REPORTED AS DISSIPATING.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED 7 MILES NORTH OF THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE. IT WAS REPORTED ALL THE WAY DOWN TO THE WATER AND A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY A TRAINED SPOTTER AT CUDJOE KEY. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT LONG KEY. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF SHOWERS MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 41 KNOTS...OR 47 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SAND KEY LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE WEATHER FLOW SENSOR AT THE KEY WEST COAST GUARD STATION. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
WATERSPOUT SITED BY TRAINED SPOTTER 217 VCNTY MILE-MARKER 89 ON THE ATLANTIC SIDE. MOVEMENT EAST AT APPROX 5 KTS.
MONROE COUNTY SHERIFF DISPATCH RELAYED THE REPORT OF A WATERSPOUT FROM THE PUBLIC NEAR MILE MARKER 88 ATLANTIC SIDE OVING SOUTHEAST AT 5 KTS.

SEVERAL PARK RANGERS AT BAHIA HONDA STATE PARK REPORTED A LARGE WATERSPOUT OVER THE ATLANTIC WATERS ABOUT 200 TO 400 FEET OFFSHORE. A VISIBLE SPRAY RING WAS EVIDENT AND THE EVENT TIME WAS ESTIMATED BETWEEN 11AM AND NOON WITH AN UNKNOWN DURATION.

SPOTTER NUMBER 217 REPORTED 1 LARGE WATERSPOUT AND 1 SMALLER WATER SPOUT ASSOCIATED WITH A CLUSTER OF SHOWERS ON THE ATLANTIC SIDE WATERS OF THE UPPER KEYS VCNTY MILE MARKER 76. MOVEMENT TO THE EAST AT 5 KTS.
NATIONAL PARK SERVICE RANGER AT FT JEFFERSON NATIONAL MONUMENT IN THE DRY TORTUGAS PHONED IN A REPORT OF A WATERSPOUT.
SOMBRERO KEY C-MAN STATION REPORTED A 39 KT WIND GUST ASSOCIATED WITH AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY FROM A CLUSTER OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVING SOUTH AT 20 KTS.
A TRAINED SPOTTERT AT MM 86 REPORTED A WATER SPOUT ALONG A CLOUD LINE NORTH NORTHWEST OF LOWER MATECUMBE. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A FORMER MONROE CO EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED A SHORT LIVED WATER SPOUT 1 NM N OF DUCK KEY...WATERSPOUT WAS DOWN TO THE HORIZON WITH NO VISIBLE SPRAY RING AND NEARLY STATIONARY.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATER SPOUT JUST NORTH OF LOWER MATECUMBE. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATER SPOUT REACDHING THE SURFACE NORTH OF LOWER MATECUMBE. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A FORMER MONROE COUNTY MANAGER REPORTED 4 WATERSPOUTS ALONG AN E/W LINE 1-2 NM SSE OF DUCK KEY. WATERSPOUTS WERE DOWN TO THE HORIZON AND ONE HAD A VISIBLE SPRAY RING.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A ROPE-LIKE WATERSPOUT LOCATED ABOUT 1 TO 2 MILES NORTH OF STOCK ISLAND. DURATION 5 MINUTES.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT LOCATED ABOUT 2 MILES NORTHWEST OF KEY WEST. THE VISIBLE FUNNEL EXTENDED ABOUT HALF WAY DOWN TO THE WATER SURFACE. DURATION 9 MINUTES.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...OUT OF THE SOUTHEAST WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT MOLASSES REEF LIGHT AT 1051 PM EDT.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 1 MILE NORTH OF BIG PINE KEY. THE VISIBLE FUNNEL EXTENDED TO THE WATER SURFACE WITH NO EVIDENT SPRAY RING. DURATION 15 MINUTES.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ONE MILE NORTH OF BIG PINE KEY. THE WATERSPOUT EXTENDED TO THE WATER SURFACE AND DURATION UNKNOWN.
COAST GUARD RELAYED A WATERSPOUT REPORT FROM A MARINER APPROXMIATELY ONE MILE SOUTH OF DUCK KEY. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A CHARTER BOAT CAPTAIN LOCATED ABOUT 5 NAUTICAL MILES SOUTHEAST OF SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT THAT EXTENDED TO THE OCEAN SURFACE FOR A SHORT PERIOD OF TIME.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...OUT OF THE SOUTH WAS MEASURED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SAND KEY LIGHT AT 1136 PM EDT.
A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...43 MPH...WAS MEASURED AT THE PULASKI LIGHT C-MAN STATION DUE TO A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
COAST GUARD RELAYED REPORT FROM INDIVIDUAL WHO REPORTED A SMALL WATERSPOUT THAT HAD ALREADY DISSIPATED NEAR ALLIGATOR REEF LIGHT. NO FURTHER DETAILS AVAILABLE.
WEATHER FLOW ANEMOMETER MEASURED A 37 KNOT TSTM WIND GUST AT THE KEY WEST COAST GUARD STATION.
A WIND GUST FROM AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY CLIPPED THE SAND KEY C-MAN STATION WITH A 34 KNOT WIND GUST. OUTFLOW BOUNDARY PROPAGATED SOUTH FROM A CLUSTER OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVING SOUTH ACROSS THE LOWER KEYS.
PARK RANGER WHO IS ALSO A COOP OBSERVER AT FORT JEFFERSON NATIONAL PARK IN THE DRY TORTUGAS REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ON 3 JULY IN THE DAILY COOP REPORT RECEIVED BY WFO KEY WEST THIS MORNING.

A 5-SECOND WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...40 MPH...FROM THE NORTH-NORTHEAST WAS RECORDED AT THE PULASKI SHOAL C-MAN STATION. ANEMOMETER HEIGHT 58 FEET ABOVE SEA LEVEL. THE WIND GUST OCCURRED IN ASSOCIATION WITH A SOUTHWESTWARD- MOVING OUTFLOW BOUNDARY INITIATED BY A THUNDERSTORM OVER THE SOUTHEASTERN GULF OF MEXICO.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A 44-KNOT THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OUT OF THE SOUTH SOUTHWEST AT 350PM LOCAL.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A 43-KNOT THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST AT 408PM LOCAL.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS AT 828PM LOCAL.
A TRAINED STORM SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 1/2 MILE WEST OF FORT ZACHARY AT 249PM LOCAL. DURATION WAS LESS THAN TWO MINUTES.

A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED OVER THE VACA KEY BIGHT APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES SOUTH OF MILE MARKER 50...OR 3 MILES WEST-SOUTHWEST OF KEY COLONY BEACH. THE WATERSPOUT OCCURRED NEAR A DEVELOPING RAIN SHOWER...AND WAS OBSERVED AS A FUNNEL CLOUD 20 PERCENT DOWN FROM CLOUD BASE...A HOLLOW CONDENSATION SLEEVE BELOW...WITH SPRAY RING AT THE WATER SURFACE. DURATION ABOUT 5 MINUTES.
A NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT LOCATED ABOUT 9 MILES NORTH OF BOCA CHICA KEY. THE VISIBLE FUNNEL EXTENDED TO THE WATER SURFACE AND HAD A DURATION OF 9 MINUTES.

THREE WATERSPOUTS LOCATED ABOUT 10 MILES NORTH OF SUGARLOAF KEY WERE REPORTED BY TRAINED SPOTTERS AND NWS EMPLOYEES. ALL WATERSPOUTS EXTENDED TO THE WATER SURFACE AND HAD DURATIONS OF 7, 9, AND 12 MINUTES.
A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS OR 43 MPH FROM THE SOUTHEAST WAS RECORDED AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION PLATFORM ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM.
A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 1 MILE NORTH OF SUGARLOAF KEY JUST BEFORE 600 PM LOCAL. A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE BUT ITS DURATION IS UNKNOWN.
AN OFF DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 2 MILES NORTH OF THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE. DURATION 4 MINUTES.
A TRAINED STORM SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT NEAR SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE NORTH-NORTHWEST WAS MEASURED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 45 KNOTS...OR 52 MPH...FROM THE EAST WAS MEASURED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH WAS MEASURED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...OR 43 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH WAS MEASURED AT THE MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING OUTFLOW BOUNDARY AND SHOWER ACTIVITY.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 1 MILE EAST OF KEY LARGO OVER THE ATLANTIC WATERS. THE WATERSPOUT WAS WELL FORMED AND EXTENDED TO THE WATER SURFACE. DURATION 12 MINUTES.
A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...OR 43 MPH...FROM THE EAST NORTHEAST WAS REPORTED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT CMAN STATION IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING CLUSTER OF THUNDERSTORMS.

AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGIST ESTIMATED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 50 MPH IN AN APPARENT MICROBURST ON BIG COPPITT KEY. A MATURE SEA GRAPE TREE WITH A 6-13 INCH DIAMETER BASE WAS TOPPLED DURING THE STORM.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...41 MPH...FROM THE EAST-NORTHEAST WAS RECORDED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION DURING A THUNDERSTORM GUST FRONT PASSAGE. ANEMOMETER HEIGHT 149 FEET ABOVE SEA LEVEL.
A BOCA CHICA WEATHER OBSERVER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT ONE MILE SOUTH OF BOCA CHICA AIR FORCE BASE. THE WATERSPOUT HAD A THIN ROPE-LIKE APPEARANCE AND LASTED APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS OR 41 MPH FROM THE SOUTH WAS RECORDED BY THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION PLATFORM DUE TO A THUNDERSTORM PASSING THROUGH.
THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A WIND GUST TO 36 KNOTS AT 650PM AS AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY PASSED THROUGH THE AREA.
THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A 34-KNOT WIND GUST OUT OF THE SOUTH AT 859PM LOCAL AS AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY PASSED THROUGH THE AREA.
SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION PLATFORM OBSERVATION RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS OR 41 MPH FROM THE SOUTH FROM A THUNDERSTORM.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATER SPOUT ESE OF DUCK KEY ON THE FRONT EDGE OF A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE. THE WATER SPOUT DESCENDED TWICE AND LASTED ROUGHLY 3 MINUTES.
A TRAINED SPOTTER ON LONG KEY OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT AT 530PM LOCAL. THE WATERSPOUT FORMED ALONG A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE THAT WAS NORTH OF THE AREA. DURATION WAS LESS THAN 15 MINUTES.

A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT WEST OF LONG KEY AT APPROXIMATELY 600PM LOCAL. THE SPOTTER VIEWED THE SPOUT FROM MILE MARKER 70 ALONG US-1 AND NOTED IT WAS MOVING TOWARDS THE NORTHEAST AWAY FROM LAND. DURATION UNKNOWN.
NAVAL AIR STATION BOCA CHICA OBSERVER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 3 MILES NNW OF THE BOCA CHICA AIRFIELD.
THE SAND KEY C-MAN STATION MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...FROM THE SOUTHEAST...WITH THE PASSAGE OF A RECENT THUNDERSTORM.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED JUST NORTH OF FLEMING KEY...WITH A CONFIRMED SPRAY RING. DURATION WAS LESS THAN 5 MINUTES.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF MOLASSES REEF...THE SPRAY RING WAS NOT VISIBLE.
A NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT A FEW MILES SOUTH OF THE KEY WEST AIRPORT. A SEPERATE OBSERVER CONFIRMED THE WATERSPOUT FROM THE UPPER AIR TOWER AT THE WFO. A SPRAY RING WAS NOT VISIBLE.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER AND AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE EACH OBSERVED A THIN WATERSPOUT OVER HAWK CHANNEL SOUTH OF BIG COPPITT KEY. DURATION 4 MINUTES.
AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE OBSERVED A BRIEF...THIN WATERSPOUT OVER HAWK CHANNEL ABOUT TWO OR THREE MILES WEST-SOUTHWEST OF FORT ZACHARY TAYLOR STATE PARK. DURATION ONE MINUTE.

A RECREATIONAL SAILOR OBSERVED A MATURE WATERSPOUT WITH A SPRAY RING ABOUT 6 MILES WEST OF SUNSET KEY. THE WATERSPOUT DEVELOPED ADJACENT TO A SHOWER WITHIN A TOWERING CUMULUS CLOUD LINE EMANATING WESTWARD OFF THE LOWER FLORIDA KEYS. DURATION 20 MINUTES.
THE AUTOMATED SURFACE OBSERVING SYSTEM AT THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT RECORDED A 36-KNOT WIND GUST AT 727PM LOCAL AS THUNDERSTORMS PASSED THROUGH THE AREA.
WEATHER OBSERVATION EQUIPMENT AT THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE OFFICE IN KEY WEST RECORDED A 34-KNOT WIND GUST AT 730PM LOCAL AS THUNDERSTORMS PASSED THROUGH THE AREA.
AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED SEVERAL FALLEN 3-INCH DIAMETER TREE LIMBS AND NEWSPAPER STAND BOXES BLOWN OVER ALONG JOLLY ROGER DRIVE ON CUDJOE KEY.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE EAST WAS MEASURED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
THE SOMBRERO KEY C-MAN STATION REPORTED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS. THE ANEMOMETER FOR THIS STATION IS LOCATED 159 FEET ABOVE MEAN SEA LEVEL.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS FROM THE EAST WAS MEASURED AT THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS FROM THE EAST WAS MEASURED AT THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 45 KNOTS FROM THE EAST WAS MEASURED AT THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION.

STRONG THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS BROUGHT DOWN SEVERAL 3 AND 4 INCH TREE BRANCHES...SNAPPED A POWER POLE...AND TWISTED TREES IN THE COMMUNITY OF TAVERNIER. TIME IS ESTIMATED FROM INFORMATION GATHERED FROM THE SPOTTER AND FROM MONROE COUNTY SHERRIFS OFFICE.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH WAS MEASURED BY THE BOCA CHICA ASOS.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED NEAR CHRISTMAS ISLAND AND OBSERVED THROUGH FLEMING ISLAND...WITH A SPRAY RING VISIBLE.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...FROM THE WEST-SOUTHWEST WAS RECORDED BY THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...FROM THE WEST WAS RECORDED BY THE SOMBRERO KEY C-MAN STATION.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH-SOUTHWEST WAS RECORDED BY THE MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION AS THUNDERSTORMS MOVED OVER THE SENSOR.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS WAS REPORTED BY THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS OR 41 MPH WAS MEASURED AT THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION PLATFORM WITH A PASSING BAND OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...FROM THE NORTHWEST WAS MEASURED BY THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT ELEVATED C-MAN STATION AS HEAVY SHOWERS PASSED OVER THE PLATFORM.
